title: Executive Operations Associate - President's Office
description: **Company Description** It all started in sunny San Diego, California in 2004 when a visionary engineer, Fred Luddy, saw the potential to transform how we work. Fast forward to today — ServiceNow stands as a global market leader, bringing innovative AI\-enhanced technology to over 8,100 customers, including 85% of the Fortune 500®. Our intelligent cloud\-based platform seamlessly connects people, systems, and processes to empower organizations to find smarter, faster, and better ways to work. But this is just the beginning of our journey. Join us as we pursue our purpose to make the world work better for everyone. **Job Description** **What you get to do in this role:** The EMEA President's Office runs on operational precision and strategic momentum. This role exists to protect both, every single day. You will work directly with the Chief of Staff, serve as the connective tissue between the executive office and cross\-functional teams, and progressively take on operational leadership as you build trust and demonstrate judgment. This is not a traditional support role. It is a high\-accountability, high\-visibility position for someone who is already an exceptional EA and is ready for more. **Executive support (approx. 25%)** * Own the Chief of Staff's calendar with sharp scrutiny, protecting strategic time and proactively resolving conflicts before they land * Manage travel, bookings, and expense reporting end\-to\-end with zero follow\-up needed * Coordinate closely with the EA to the EMEA President to ensure non\-negotiable meeting alignment * Act as a reliable, discreet first point of contact for operational and administrative matters **Business Operations \& Project Management (approx. 75%)** * Set up and maintain President's Office operating cadences: weekly syncs, leadership forums, cross\-functional checkpoints * Track actions, milestones, and blockers across multiple workstreams, escalating proactively before issues become problems * Own the end\-to\-end planning and execution of major EMEA events: QBRs, SKO, Knowledge, Rise Together, president\-led office visits * Build and maintain SharePoint pages, OneNote structures, trackers, and insight dashboards the team actually uses * Support the development of executive\-ready presentations and briefing materials * Reinforce connection points between central and regional teams, ensuring operational clarity across a matrixed organization **Qualifications** **To be successful in this role you have:** * 5\+ years as an Executive Assistant in a fast\-paced tech or SaaS environment, ideally supporting C\-suite or a President's Office * A track record of reaching beyond your title: you have led projects, built systems, or taken ownership of things nobody asked you to own * You have felt frustrated when your role was purely logistical, and you have done something about it * Highly organized with strong written and verbal English, and the confidence to communicate with senior stakeholders directly * Fluent in SharePoint, OneNote, Excel, PowerPoint, and Smartsheet or equivalent * Global mindset and genuine curiosity about how a large, matrixed organization operates **Additional Information** **Work Personas** We approach our distributed world of work with flexibility and trust. Work personas (flexible, remote, or required in office) are categories that are assigned to ServiceNow employees depending on the nature of their work and their assigned work location. Learn more here. To determine eligibility for a work persona, ServiceNow may confirm the distance between your primary residence and the closest ServiceNow office using a third\-party service. **Equal Opportunity Employer** ServiceNow is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, national origin or nationality, ancestry, age, disability, gender identity or expression, marital status, veteran status, or any other category protected by law. In addition, all qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with legal requirements. **Accommodations** We strive to create an accessible and inclusive experience for all candidates. If you require a reasonable accommodation to complete any part of the application process, or are unable to use this online application and need an alternative method to apply, please contact globaltalentss@servicenow.com for assistance. **Export Control Regulations** For positions requiring access to controlled technology subject to export control regulations, including the U.S. Export Administration Regulations (EAR), ServiceNow may be required to obtain export control approval from government authorities for certain individuals. All employment is contingent upon ServiceNow obtaining any export license or other approval that may be required by relevant export control authorities. From Fortune. ©2025 Fortune Media IP Limited. All rights reserved. Used under license.

title: FP&A Analyst
description: A leading global investment manager is building out its Finance team and this role sits right at the centre of it. This is not a back\-office reporting role. It’s a commercial, forward\-looking FP\&A seat with real visibility to senior leadership and direct involvement in decision making. **The Role** * Own monthly financial dashboards for senior management * Analyse revenue, cost drivers and KPIs to improve forecasting accuracy * Partner with business leaders on budgeting and strategic initiatives * Build financial models to support product expansion and growth decisions * Work cross\-functionally with Risk, Strategy and HR on firm wide projects * Support financial reporting as required **The Opportunity** * Newly created team in Dublin \- genuine opportunity to shape and influence * Direct exposure to global FP\&A teams * Strong blend of commercial FP\&A \+ technical finance / reporting * High visibility role with interaction at senior stakeholder level * Fast\-paced, high\-performance environment where strong analysts thrive **Requirements** * 4\+ years FP\&A experience (financial services preferred) * Strong Excel (VBA / macros a plus) and analytical skillset * Ability to communicate clearly and influence stakeholders * Comfortable operating in a fast\-moving, high\-pressure environment

title: FP&A Data Analyst
description: **Why Toshiba Global Commerce Solutions?** At Toshiba, we're transforming retail with innovative solutions that tackle everyday challenges. Our mission is to turn retail visions into reality by providing expert guidance and cutting\-edge technologies. We help Retailers navigate obstacles and accelerate their growth, reimagining customer engagement. Innovation drives retail success, so we invest in future technologies to keep pace with evolving customer expectations. We work closely with clients to develop strategies and solutions that align with their goals, ensuring their business shines today and in the future. Join Toshiba Global Commerce Solutions and be a key partner in our customers' success. If you share our vision of a seamless, interactive shopping experience, let's drive this together! **JOB TITLE:** FP\&A Data Analyst **DEPARTMENT:** EU Finance Organisation **LOCATION: Ireland or Leeds, UK.** **Role Overview \- What’s ‘In\-store’?** The FP\&A Data Analyst is a critical contributor to the European FP\&A function, responsible for delivering high\-quality data analysis, reporting, and insight to support financial planning, performance management, and commercial decision making. This role is highly data and process driven, with a strong emphasis on automation, data integrity, and modern reporting to strengthen forecasting, improve data governance, and enhance the analytical capability of the finance organisation. **What Success Looks Like** * Automated, accurate, and scalable reporting that improves decision\-making. * Strong data foundations to enable improved forecasting and performance management. * Clear, actionable insights delivered consistently to senior leadership. * Measurable improvements in reporting efficiency, data governance, and process standardisation. **Key Responsibilities:** **Financial Planning \& Forecasting** * Support budgeting, forecasting, and long\-range planning cycles * Prepare, validate, and analyse large data sets to support modelling and scenario planning * Deliver variance analysis, trend insights, and performance commentary * Consolidate multi\-country inputs into a clear financial view **Reporting \& Data Visualisation** * Develop, automate, and maintain dashboards and reports using **advanced Power BI** * Ensure accuracy, consistency, and integrity of financial and operational data. * Modernize legacy reporting by implementing scalable, insight driven visualisation tools. * Partner with cross functional teams to understand reporting needs and translate them into effective BI solutions. **Data Management \& Process Improvement** * Strengthen data governance, documentation and standardisation across FP\&A processes * Identify opportunities to streamline data flows and automate manual reporting tasks. * Integrate and analyse **Salesforce** data to enhance commercial and financial insights. * Support continuous improvement initiatives to enhance forecasting accuracy and reporting efficiency. **Business Partnering \& Insight Generation** * Deliver analytical support to senior stakeholders. * Translate complex data into clear, actionable insights that support strategic and operational decisions. * Build strong relationships within Finance and Operations teams to ensure alignment on KPIs and data definitions. **What We’re Looking For** * 3–5\+ years in FP\&A, Data Analytics, Business Intelligence, or similar analytical finance roles * Experience in multinational or matrix environments is preferred * Proven ability to automate reporting, improve processes and data quality. * Experience working with Salesforce data is highly desirable. **Technical Skills** * Advanced Power BI capability (data modelling, DAX, dashboards, automation) * Strong Excel skills (Power Query, Power Pivot, advanced modelling) * Experience with ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Workday) * Strong understanding of financial statements, KPIs, and FP\&A processes **Leadership \& Behavioural Competencies** * Effective communicator able to simplify complex data for nontechnical audiences. * Structured, analytical, and detail\-oriented mindset **Education \& Qualifications** * Degree in Finance, Accounting, Analytics, or related field * *Professional certifications (ACCA, CIMA, CFA) or BI/data certifications are advantageous* **EEO** Toshiba is an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer that evaluates qualified applicants without regard to protected characteristics. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Individuals who may require reasonable adjustments because of a disability for any part of the employment process should email pollyanna.hilton@toshibagcs.com to inform of any requests. **Diversity, Equity \& Inclusion** We at Toshiba Global Commerce Solutions firmly believe that our people are an integral part to the success of our customers. Furthermore, we’re committed to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion for all our people as highlighted by our 5 Core Principles (Create Outreach, Foster Belonging, Unleash Opportunity, Diverse Cultural Engagement and Culture of Transparency). With over 50\+ years of commitment to retail, we are RETAIL. Our RETAIL values (respect, empowerment, teamwork, adaptability, leadership) characterize what our employees and clients can expect when they work with us every time and all the time. We’re passionate about our customers the retail industry and becoming a more responsible company as we help create a brighter future.

title: Finance Analyst Newly Qualified
company: Coopman Search and Selection | B Corp™
description: **Financial Analyst – Newly Qualified / 1–3 Years PQE** **Sector:** Asset \& Wealth Management / Aircraft Leasing We are currently working with a leading financial services organisation in Dublin that is looking to hire a **Financial Analyst** to join their growing finance team. This is an excellent opportunity for a **newly qualified accountant or someone with 1–3 years’ post\-qualification experience** , ideally coming from **audit or a financial services background** , particularly within **asset \& wealth management or aircraft leasing** . This role offers strong exposure to senior stakeholders, commercial finance activities, and the opportunity to move from practice into a highly regarded industry environment. **Key Responsibilities** * Support the preparation and review of **monthly and quarterly financial reporting** * Assist with **financial analysis, budgeting, and forecasting** * Work closely with senior finance stakeholders on **performance analysis and business insights** * Support **financial modelling and variance analysis** * Assist with **regulatory and statutory reporting where required** * Contribute to **process improvements and finance transformation initiatives** **Requirements** * **ACA / ACCA / CPA qualified** (or recently qualified) * **0–3 years PQE** with experience in **audit or financial services** * Exposure to **asset management, wealth management, aircraft leasing, or wider financial services** is highly desirable * Strong **analytical and Excel skills** * Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities **What’s on Offer** * Strong career progression within a reputable organisation * Excellent exposure to commercial finance * Competitive salary and benefits package * Hybrid working environment in Dublin If you are a **recently qualified accountant looking to move into industry** , or a **finance professional with early PQE seeking the next step** , please apply or reach out for a confidential discussion.

title: Financial Analyst
description: **We are seeking a Financial Analyst to join a growing organisation operating within the energy and infrastructure sector, to support a portfolio of large\-scale projects. The business is operating in a capital\-intensive environment and continues to expand, offering strong exposure across project delivery, commercial decision\-making, and senior stakeholder engagement.** **Key Responsibilities:** * Partner with project and senior stakeholders to provide financial insight, challenge assumptions, and support decision\-making. * Support budgeting, forecasting, and business case development, including scenario analysis and cost evaluation. * Prepare and review monthly management accounts, including variance analysis and clear performance commentary. * Maintain strong financial controls and support audit, compliance, and governance requirements across projects. * Contribute to financial planning, cashflow forecasting, and the ongoing development of finance processes. **Key Requirements:** * Qualified accountant (ACA / ACCA / CIMA or equivalent) with approximately 1–3 years’ post\-qualification experience. * Experience in a finance business partnering or commercially focused finance role. * Background in a capital\-intensive environment such as energy, infrastructure, construction, or property. * Strong experience in management accounting, budgeting, forecasting, and financial analysis. * Confident communicator with the ability to engage and challenge non\-finance stakeholders. **This role offers the opportunity to develop business partnering experience within a commercial finance environment, supporting key projects within a Joint Venture alongside major industry organisations.** For a confidential discussion, please contact Matthew Fleming at mfleming@elevatepartners.ie.

title: Financial Data Analyst, Technical Operations
description: Who we are **About Stripe** Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies \- from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups \- use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. **About The Team** The Payments organization owns some of Stripe’s most critical payment flows and a platform that processes hundreds of billions of dollars in payments a year. Our team is responsible for translating complex partner specifications related to network costs (interchange and scheme fees) into simplified logic for internal and external consumption. This also drives decisions and product recommendations to manage the underlying network costs paid by Stripe and our users. The team partners closely with the engineering, product, finance, and partnership groups to manage and understand Stripe’s network costs. Our work is core to Stripe’s business, as Technical Operations roles in Payments are a dynamic and key component of Stripe's success. We sit at the intersection of product/platform engineers and financial partners, connecting them to ensure that everyone thrives and nothing is lost in translation. What you’ll do We are looking to add payment enthusiasts who enjoy interpreting complex cost structures and ever changing payment network systems in order to optimize on behalf of Stripe and our users. You will be instrumental in building Stripe’s approach to managing our global network cost base. **Responsibilities** * Collaborate across the company, including engineering, accounting, financial partnerships and product teams, to analyze billions of dollars moving through the Stripe platform. * Translate network specifications into implementation instructions to create billing models for new and revised fees (both scheme and interchange) * Influence product strategy to minimize network costs * Work with Product and Engineering to implement cost saving opportunities * Partner with Finance to prioritize areas for investment and explain trends in costs and margin impact * Monitor and maintain the accuracy of the fees through validation and alerting * Creating fee explainability to internal and external stakeholders * Hosting education sessions for internal teams to scale knowledge * Introduce data and process efficiencies to meet the scale and speed of Stripe. Identify and automate inefficient operational processes to reduce manual workloads * Scale our network cost operation by driving the design, documentation, and training of operational processes to leverage partner relationships. Who you are We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. **Minimum Requirements** * Have 5\+ years of in Payments at a leading technology or payments company * Have experience writing SQL to query relational databases * Have experience managing communications and workflows with technical and non\-technical teams across internal and external stakeholders * Experience mentoring junior team members in technical skill development, operational strategy and problem solving * Have experience navigating the nuanced complexity of financial systems and solving large scale, technical challenges * Have strong financial modeling and analytical skills to investigate, prioritize, and identify the root cause of issues * Have experience managing multiple, concurrent work streams, identifying and triaging technical issues and driving solutions with clear metrics to show impact In\-office expectations Office\-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico and Bengaluru, India work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in\-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in\-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible. Pay and benefits The annual salary range for this role in the primary location is €84,000 \- €126,000\. This range may change if you are hired in another location. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings (“OTE”) range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and specific location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the primary location may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process. Specific benefits and details about what compensation is included in the salary range listed above will vary depending on the applicant’s location and can be discussed in more detail during the interview process. Benefits/additional compensation for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; retirement plans; health benefits; and wellness stipends.

title: GenAI Developer (MERN / MEAN Fullstack)
description: We are building an Engineering Team in Dublin focused on rapid prototyping and innovation. We seek a hands\-on, delivery\-oriented Full Stack Engineer for our Product Engineering. In this role, you will engage in the development and implementation of innovative solutions that leverage Generative AI and other advanced technologies. You will provide technical leadership across the full stack, setting standards for robust design and timely delivery of quality, tested code. **Job Title: Innovation Engineer / Gen AI Developer** **Team Build \- Extensive hiring** **Length of Assignment: 6 Months, long project with likely extensions** **Location: Dublin, Ireland (2 days in the office per week)** **Working Days/Hours: Monday – Friday (35 hours per week: 9:30 AM – 5:30 PM)** **Day Rate: 600 – 700 Euros per day (Umbrella) or Permanent 60\-110k Euros** **What You Need To Have** * 5\+ years of commercial experience with a proven track record of delivering scalable digital products. * Ability to design systems across the full stack. * Deep knowledge of design patterns, tools, and techniques for delivering high\-performance, secure UI applications. * Strong experience with React \+ Tailwind / Next.js or equivalent frameworks. * Proficiency in Node.js (Nest.js); Python is a bonus. * Strong understanding of DOM, HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, and responsive web design patterns. * Experience with data integration and working with JSON and RESTful APIs. * Strong debugging and troubleshooting skills. * Knowledge of automated testing strategies and best practices. * Familiarity with management/tracking tools such as JIRA/Confluence. * Experience working in an Agile environment. **The Soft Skills We Expect You To Have** * A results\-driven mindset, focused on delivering outcomes efficiently. * Proactive and driven, always seeking new opportunities and challenges. * Strong agency and critical thinking skills to lead engineering efforts and deliver value to end users. * Initiative to work independently without constant supervision. * Leadership qualities, taking charge when necessary. * Full responsibility for tasks, seeing them through to completion. * Confidence in abilities with effective communication skills. * Exceptional verbal communication skills, able to articulate ideas clearly. * Strong comprehension of complex concepts. * Comfortable presenting ideas or solutions in front of an audience. * Ability to lead design sessions, think end\-to\-end through solutions, and ask the right questions. * Persistence in problem\-solving, making multiple attempts before seeking assistance. * Ability to mentor and coach junior team members. **What Technical Skills Do We Require** * MEAN / MERN Stack experience. * Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS / GCP / Azure). * Exposure to Apigee as an API Gateway. **What Would Be Nice for You to Have Experience With:** * Event\-driven, asynchronous, and reactive programming patterns. * Secure coding and API development practices. * Microservices design and best practices. * Unidirectional data flow patterns (Redux, etc.). * RxJS or other reactive programming techniques/libraries. * GitHub, including best practices around branching strategies. * Knowledge of AI technologies (OpenAI, ChatGPT 4\.0, Gemini, Claude). **What Makes You Stand Out** * A technical expert with a personal curiosity for technology, trends, and standards—someone that other engineers want to collaborate with. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, comfortable working in a distributed environment, with the ability to build strong relationships across teams, locations, and time zones. * A strong “product mindset” with the ability to envision, articulate, and execute a roadmap for delivering impactful solutions. **Note:** This is a wishlist of skills and experiences. We are looking for strong developers who are ready to adapt to a quickly changing tech landscape, working on projects that may last only a few weeks. If you are excited about innovation and eager to tackle new challenges, we encourage you to apply! Robert Walters Operations Limited is an employment business and employment agency and welcomes applications from all candidates

INDLP

title: Global Fund Registration Officer
description: OVERVIEW The Maples Group is a standard bearer in financial and legal services, trusted by many of the world’s largest hedge fund managers, private equity firms and international corporations. Our distinction flows from our carefully curated team: 2,500\+ professionals characterised by tenacity, ethics and exacting excellence. Operating in key financial centres across the Americas, Asia, Europe and the Middle East, our international presence offers a unique springboard for career development and cross\-cultural immersion. The Maples Group looks to add a Dublin based Global Fund Registration Officer to our Global Registration Services (GRS) team and invites eager and qualified candidates to apply. We are committed to diversity, inclusion and equality of opportunity as we attract, retain and develop world\-class talent. Role Title \& Overview The GRS team is a specialist client\-facing practice operating at the intersection of investment funds law, regulatory compliance, and cross\-border distribution. Sitting within a leading Irish funds and investment management practice, the GRS team assists clients with the practical, regulatory, compliance, and administrative requirements associated with marketing and listing investment funds globally. The team comprises specialist legal, regulatory, and compliance professionals who deliver a fully integrated and coordinated approach to fund distribution across multiple jurisdictions, with a particular focus on Europe. We are seeking a Fund Registration Officer to join this established and expanding team. This is a rewarding, specialist role ideally suited to an organised, detail\-oriented professional with an interest in cross\-border fund distribution, regulatory filings, and the evolving European and international regulatory landscape. The Fund Registration Officer will play an important supporting role in ensuring that clients' investment fund products, including UCITS and AIFs, are registered, maintained, and compliant across the jurisdictions in which they are distributed. The position reports to a Registrations Advisor, Executive, or Associate and forms part of a wider team of GRS professionals. This is a genuine opportunity to develop deep expertise in a niche area of investment funds regulation, one that is of ever\-increasing commercial importance, and to build a meaningful career within a global legal services environment. Key Responsibilities * Assist with the preparation, coordination, and submission of regulatory filings to home and host national competent authorities, including fund passporting notifications, new product registrations, updated fund documentation ensuring all filings are completed accurately and within regulatory deadlines. * Liaise with clients, local lawyers, translators, service providers, regulators, and stock exchanges on various ongoing registration and distribution\-related matters, ensuring deliverables are provided to multiple parties within specified timeframes. * Maintain and update all GRS systems, records, and internal databases, ensuring that registration data across all jurisdictions is current and accurate. * Support the preparation and handling of legal documentation associated with fund registrations, including facilitating the production of documentation and monitoring ongoing obligations. * Maintain, update, and ensure consistency across GRS templates, precedent documentation, and know\-how materials. * Conduct due diligence on regulatory requirements across all countries where GRS clients are registered, supporting senior team members in ensuring that correct procedures are in place. * Support the establishment of new or changed product structures and solutions across multiple jurisdictions, including assisting with the coordination of regulatory and administrative steps required. * Assist with the preparation of billing narratives, work\-in\-progress reports, and general administrative and file management tasks in accordance with firm guidelines and the parameters of client engagements. * Support adherence to the firm's compliance and data protection standards. * Provide proactive assistance to the GRS team and clients on other fund distribution\-related issues as they arise. Required Skills \& Experience * A minimum of one to two years' experience working in a paralegal, regulatory, compliance, or legal support role within the investment funds or financial services sector; experience within a law firm, management company, administrator, or professional services environment is desirable. * A working knowledge of, or demonstrable interest in, the laws and regulations applicable to financial services and, in particular, the cross\-border distribution of investment funds in Ireland, Luxembourg, and other European jurisdictions. * Familiarity with UCITS and AIFMD frameworks, fund passporting procedures, and regulatory notification processes is an advantage but not essential, a willingness to learn in this area is key. * Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines across different jurisdictions simultaneously. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English; a second European language is desirable. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, with strong skills in Excel in particular. * A graduate degree in law, finance, business, or a related discipline is preferred. Personal Attributes * Highly organised and methodical, with strong attention to detail and the ability to work accurately across complex, multi\-jurisdictional processes. * A self\-starter with a genuine interest in developing specialist knowledge of cross\-border fund distribution and regulatory compliance. * A dependable and conscientious team player who takes pride in the quality and accuracy of their work. * Flexible, hard\-working, and disciplined, with the ability to perform under pressure and to tight deadlines. * Confident and professional in dealing with colleagues, clients, and regulatory authorities. * Proactive in identifying issues, asking questions, and suggesting improvements to processes and workflows. * Possesses a strong sense of duty and responsibility. What the Role Offers This is a unique opportunity to join a well\-established, market\-leading GRS practice and to develop a career in a specialist, commercially important area of investment funds regulation. Cross\-border fund registration is a growing and dynamic field, and the Officer role offers direct exposure to a wide range of regulatory frameworks, jurisdictions, and high\-profile client relationships from an early stage. As part of a supportive and collegial team, you will benefit from structured mentoring and day\-to\-day knowledge sharing with experienced colleagues, with clear scope for professional growth as the team and practice continue to expand. The role sits within the full resources of a global law firm's network, providing an excellent platform from which to build a rewarding long\-term career in this niche discipline. ABOUT MAPLES GROUP Over five decades, the Maples Group has grown from modest beginnings into one of the world's preeminent professional services firms, offering specialised fiduciary, fund administration, regulatory and compliance, entity formation and management and legal services on the laws of the British Virgin Islands, the Cayman Islands, Ireland, Jersey and Luxembourg. You can learn more about the Maples Group on our corporate website. Experience our culture and our people on our Careers Page or on LinkedIn. Please note that it is Maples standard policy to undertake various background screening checks on all applicants to whom a conditional job offer is made. All job offers are subject to the completion of satisfactory background screening checks. Further details on the background screening checks will be advised to each candidate who receives a conditional job offer from Maples. Disclaimer: All personal information collected during the application process will be used for recruitment\-related purposes only. Please refer to our Job Applicant Privacy Notice at maples.com/privacy for details on how we handle personal information relating to job applicants.

title: Graduate (EHS)
description: Job Description: IPS is hiring! We are looking for a talented **Graduate EHS** to join our industry\-leading team at our European office, located in **Dublin, Ireland**. Join our team and become part of our enriching Graduate Programme. This role presents a unique opportunity to experience client interaction and on\-site presence, minimizing desk\-based tasks through active engagement with clients and construction sites. As a member of our team, you'll collaborate within a diverse and inclusive group of Designers, Architects, Construction professionals, and Engineers. In this transformative two\-year programme your focus will be on the technical and practical aspects of projects, with a particular emphasis on those related to the Pharmaceutical and Life Sciences industry. As a participant in our Graduate Programme, you'll receive mentorship support, and comprehensive training in technical and non\-technical skills. Engage with a supportive cohort of graduates and lay the foundation for a successful and fulfilling career. IPS values diversity and inclusion, and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply and bring their unique perspectives to our collaborative and innovative work environment. Join us and take the first steps toward a future of professional growth and achievement. The hiring team will review applications on a rolling basis and reach out to applicants selected for the next steps in our hiring process. As an Environment, Health \& Safety Graduate, you will support the EHS Manager and team to promote and create a culture of an incident and injury\-free workplace for everyone. What you’ll do:* Support EHS Team in developing and implementing safety plans * Assist with Safety Metric reporting. * Maintain EHS documentation and upcoming trainings to be fully compliant. * Assist with IPS project specific site Inductions, PPE adherence, SOR/SPA, method statement training when required. IPS offers exciting growth opportunities! Come join a caring, nimble team that delivers world class designs for our pharmaceutical clients and take your career to new heights. Qualifications \& Requirements: * Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Construction Management, or a related discipline or an equivalent technical degree. * Personal Development Focused. A team player with strong time management skills * Time Management, Independent Working * Effective Communication * Collaboration \& Teamwork * Safety Focus * Passion for Results All interviews are conducted either in person or virtually, with **video required**. About Us: **IPS**, a Berkshire Hathaway company, is a global leader in technical consulting, architecture, engineering, procurement, construction management, commissioning, qualification, and validation services for technically complex facilities. **Integration** is how we think and work. It requires the ability to unite existing and emerging technologies into a process that reliably delivers products and accelerates speed to market. That end\-to\-end mindset allows us to be agile and anticipate challenges as we move projects toward operational readiness. IPS applies design and delivery expertise, client\-side experience, and supplier\-side insight to every project. We infuse our curiosity, our passion, and our commitment to partnership in delivering solutions at any scale. We meet our clients where they are and get them to where they need to go. That’s only possible when we care as much as our clients do. Specialties Process Architecture, Process Design, Engineering, Construction, Commissioning, Qualification, LEAN Project Delivery, Strategic Master Planning, Energy Conservation Management, Project Controls, Program/Project Management, Operations/Logistics Strategy and Planning, EPCMV, and Staffing Services. *All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment at IPS without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, sex, age, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic predisposition or carrier status, veteran, marital, or citizenship status, or any other status protected by law. IPS is proud to be an affirmative action and equal opportunity employer.*

title: Graduate (EHS)
description: **Job Description** IPS is hiring! We are looking for a talented **Graduate EHS** to join our industry\-leading team at our European office, located in **Dublin, Ireland** . Join our team and become part of our enriching Graduate Programme. This role presents a unique opportunity to experience client interaction and on\-site presence, minimizing desk\-based tasks through active engagement with clients and construction sites. As a member of our team, you'll collaborate within a diverse and inclusive group of Designers, Architects, Construction professionals, and Engineers. In this transformative two\-year programme your focus will be on the technical and practical aspects of projects, with a particular emphasis on those related to the Pharmaceutical and Life Sciences industry. As a participant in our Graduate Programme, you'll receive mentorship support, and comprehensive training in technical and non\-technical skills. Engage with a supportive cohort of graduates and lay the foundation for a successful and fulfilling career. IPS values diversity and inclusion, and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply and bring their unique perspectives to our collaborative and innovative work environment. Join us and take the first steps toward a future of professional growth and achievement. The hiring team will review applications on a rolling basis and reach out to applicants selected for the next steps in our hiring process. As an Environment, Health \& Safety Graduate, you will support the EHS Manager and team to promote and create a culture of an incident and injury\-free workplace for everyone. **What You’ll Do** * Support EHS Team in developing and implementing safety plans * Assist with Safety Metric reporting. * Maintain EHS documentation and upcoming trainings to be fully compliant. * Assist with IPS project specific site Inductions, PPE adherence, SOR/SPA, method statement training when required. IPS offers exciting growth opportunities! Come join a caring, nimble team that delivers world class designs for our pharmaceutical clients and take your career to new heights. **Qualifications \& Requirements** * Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Construction Management, or a related discipline or an equivalent technical degree. * Personal Development Focused. A team player with strong time management skills * Time Management, Independent Working * Effective Communication * Collaboration \& Teamwork * Safety Focus * Passion for Results All interviews are conducted either in person or virtually, with **video required** . **About Us** **IPS** , a Berkshire Hathaway company, is a global leader in technical consulting, architecture, engineering, procurement, construction management, commissioning, qualification, and validation services for technically complex facilities. **Integration** is how we think and work. It requires the ability to unite existing and emerging technologies into a process that reliably delivers products and accelerates speed to market. That end\-to\-end mindset allows us to be agile and anticipate challenges as we move projects toward operational readiness. IPS applies design and delivery expertise, client\-side experience, and supplier\-side insight to every project. We infuse our curiosity, our passion, and our commitment to partnership in delivering solutions at any scale. We meet our clients where they are and get them to where they need to go. That’s only possible when we care as much as our clients do. Specialties Process Architecture, Process Design, Engineering, Construction, Commissioning, Qualification, LEAN Project Delivery, Strategic Master Planning, Energy Conservation Management, Project Controls, Program/Project Management, Operations/Logistics Strategy and Planning, EPCMV, and Staffing Services. *All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment at IPS without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, sex, age, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic predisposition or carrier status, veteran, marital, or citizenship status, or any other status protected by law. IPS is proud to be an affirmative action and equal opportunity employer.* Connect With Us! If you do not meet the qualifications for this specific role, but would like to submit your resume for general consideration, please click here!

title: Graduate - AI
description: **Graduate AI** **About the Role** A Graduate AI Agent Developer supports the design and development of intelligent, automated agents that streamline processes and enhance decision making across the organisation. The role focuses on turning real business problems into scalable AI\-driven solutions that deliver measurable efficiency and improved user experiences. Working closely with technical and operational teams, the developer ensures that agents are reliable, compliant, and aligned to organisational goals. This role adds value by accelerating digital transformation and enabling teams to work smarter through safe and effective solutions. **What You Will Learn \& Deliver** * Learns how to gather practical business requirements and understand the day to day responsibilities of different roles throughout the business. * Learns how to design, build, and refine AI agents that address real operational challenges across the organisation. * Develops practical skills in prompt engineering, data handling, and responsible AI practices while working with experienced teams. * Delivers reliable and well tested AI agents that enhance workflow efficiency and support informed decision making. * Contributes to measurable business value by automating manual processes and improving overall service quality. **Required Skills \& Knowledge** * A foundational understanding/interest in artificial intelligence concepts, including large language models, prompt engineering, data handling, and responsible AI principles, with the ability to apply them in practical scenarios. * Knowledge Basic programming or scripting capability, along with the ability to work with APIs, structured data, and workflow automation tools. * Analytical and problem solving skills, with the ability to break down business processes and identify where AI agents can add value. * Good communication and documentation skills, ensuring clear handover of logic, requirements, and agent behaviour to technical and non technical stakeholders. * A willingness to learn modern AI platforms such as Copilot Studio, Azure AI, or equivalent low code and pro code tools, and to stay updated on safe, compliant AI development practices. \[Manager Guidance: Only include essentials. Over\-specification risks excluding candidates.] **Education \& Qualifications** A relevant degree OR equivalent practical experience (placements, internships, project work). For regulated roles, specify accredited programmes as required. Any 3rd level qualification would be suitable if the candidate displayed the desired skills above, however typically you would often see digital development graduates coming from degrees like; Business, Business Information Systems, Computer Science, Data Analytics or Psychology. **Uniphar – Who we are** With a workforce of more than 3000 employees spread across Ireland, United Kingdom, Mainland Europe, MENA, and the USA, Uniphar is a trusted global partner to pharma and MedTech manufacturers, working to improve patient access to medicines around the world. Uniphar provides outsourced and specialized services to its clients, leveraging the strong relationships with 200\+ of the world’s best known pharmaco\-medical manufacturers across multiple geographies, enabled by our cutting\-edge digital technology and our highly expert teams. Uniphar is organised into three key divisions: Supply Chain Retail \& On Demand, Uniphar Med Tech and Uniphar Pharma. **Supply Chain \& Retail** Supply Chain Services provides both pre\-wholesale and wholesale distribution of pharmaceutical, healthcare and animal health products to pharmacies and hospital nationwide. The business supports a diverse customer base through the provision of strong service levels coupled with innovative commercial initiatives. In addition, the business provides services and supports that help independent community pharmacy to compete more effectively in an increasingly difficult environment and runs a network of Uniphar\-owned pharmacies under the Life, Allcare, McCauleys and Hickeys symbol brands. **Pharma – On Demand** The On\-Demand business is a specialist medical supply business that sources and distributes pharmaceuticals, medical equipment, and consumable supplies to healthcare professionals in over 160 countries worldwide. We have offices in Ireland, UK, Europe, USA and New Zealand. The company’s main divisions encompass everything from International Sales through Sales to NGO’s and Charities, Clinical Trials Supply, Imported (Unlicensed Medicines), Sexual Health Supplies, and the storage and distribution of medical products and devices for third parties**.** Click here to find out more: About Us \- Who we are \| Uniphar **Culture at Uniphar** Uniphar is a combination of many businesses each with its own identity, culture, values, and ways of working. Our overarching culture narrative is inclusive of each of these stores. Uniphar lives and breathes four key pillars which unpin and support our culture. We have a people first approach \- we do the right thing. We support entrepreneurial spirit where adaptability, commitment \& resilience is embodied in our way of working. We have a Common Purpose that connects our diversified businesses and people. Finally, Trust is at the heart of how we operate. Everything Uniphar does is enabled by our people and as we continue to grow domestically and internationally, we become more diverse. This diversity fuels our business and culture. **Sustainability** Sustainability is at the core of what we do and is deeply embedded in our business strategy. We want to contribute positively to the people and the world around us. Running our business in a sustainable way ensures that Uniphar will continue to prosper in the long\-term. We are focused on ensuring that each of the five pillars of our sustainability strategy are a fundamental part of our decision\-making process.

title: Graduate - HR & Analytics
description: We are partnering with a leading financial services organisation to hire an HR Graduate to join their Dublin team. This is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate or early\-career professional with strong technical and analytical skills, who is interested in building a career in HR, analytics, and systems. **Salary:** €40,000 **Location:** Dublin 4 **About the Role** This role sits within a small, collaborative HR team and offers broad exposure to the full employee life cycle. You’ll support day\-to\-day HR operations while also working with data, systems, and reporting to help improve how HR delivers value to the business. It’s an ideal role for someone who enjoys working with **Excel, data, and technology** , and wants to apply those skills in a people\-focused environment. **What You’ll Do** * Support HR administration across the employee life cycle * Maintain accurate data in HR systems and ensure data quality * Assist with recruitment coordination and onboarding * Support payroll and benefits administration * Prepare reports, dashboards, and presentations (including board materials) * Analyse HR data to identify trends and support decision\-making * Contribute to improving and automating HR processes **What We’re Looking For** * A recent graduate (or 0–2 years’ experience) in HR, Business, Analytics, or a related field * Strong technical skills – particularly **Excel (pivot tables)** ; exposure to Power BI or similar tools is a plus * Interest in HR, people analytics, or HR systems * Strong attention to detail and organisational skills * Confident communication skills, including presentations * Proactive, curious, and eager to learn *By applying to this job, your CV will be assessed by a consultant from Recruiters.ie for the position described and your CV may be added to our secure applicant tracking system however your details will never be shared to any other third party without your consent.*

title: Graduate Associate
description: Job Description This is a unique opportunity to be a part of something extraordinary. You will work on a variety of interesting, challenging projects and work streams that will see you interact with different areas of our business building your personal brand and engaging with a wide range of stakeholder’s, preparing you for a leadership role. If you are up for the challenge, please submit your application asap. An Extraordinary Opportunity Awaits …. Responsibilities Do You Have What It Takes? * Recently graduated (or expect to graduate) with a minimum 2\.1 bachelor’s or master’s degree in any discipline * Mobile and willing to travel * Strong work ethic with the ambition to succeed * Energetic, passionate and flexible with a resilient and positive, can\-do attitude * A proven track record of achievement * Team player * Committed to daa values * Qualifications What We Can Offer You: * Exciting and Diverse International Career Opportunities * Competitive starting salary with a Performance Related Bonus * 2 year Programme offering Personal Development, Mentoring and Coaching * Cycle To Work and Tax Saver Schemes * Excellent Pension Scheme \- Life Assurance and Income Protection Plan * Discounted Shopping across our retail outlets * On\-Site Sports Facilities \& Health and Wellness initiatives

title: Graduate Digital Trust & Community Analyst – Serbian, Estonian & Norwegian Speakers
description: Graduate Digital Trust \& Community Analyst – Serbian, Estonian \& Norwegian Speakers **Location:** Dublin, Ireland **Contract:** Full\-time, 40 hours/week, 12\-month contract (with potential extension) **Work Hours:** Rotational shifts in a 24/7 operation About the Role Are you a recent graduate or early\-career professional fluent in **Serbian, Estonian, or Norwegian** ? Join a **leading global tech company** as a **Graduate Digital Trust \& Community Analyst** and help create safer, more welcoming online communities. In this role, you’ll gain hands\-on experience reviewing user\-generated content, investigating reports, and contributing to policies that protect millions of users worldwide. You’ll be part of a supportive, international team based in Dublin, with opportunities for mentorship, training, and career growth. Key Responsibilities * Review and assess user\-generated content (text, images, video) to ensure compliance with platform standards * Investigate reports and take action in line with company policies * Respond to user inquiries clearly and professionally * Support research and analysis to improve content moderation strategies * Contribute to team goals for quality, accuracy, and productivity Skills \& Qualifications * Fluency in **Serbian, Estonian, or Norwegian** AND English * Strong attention to detail and good judgment * Curious, proactive, and eager to learn * Comfortable working with digital platforms and online tools * Recent graduates or those with some experience in content moderation, digital operations, or related fields What You’ll Gain * Hands\-on training and ongoing mentorship in online safety and digital operations * Experience in a globally recognized tech company * Exposure to international best practices in content moderation and community management * Clear career progression opportunities * Collaborative, inclusive, and multicultural work environment If you’re motivated, detail\-oriented, and ready to **launch your career in digital safety** , apply now via **Emily Mason** .

title: Graduate Electrical Services Engineers 2026
description: **Graduate Electrical Services Engineer 2026** **Position** : Electrical Services Graduate Engineers \- 2026 **Location** : Dublin, Kildare, Cork, Limerick, Gavle, Copenhagen, Malmo, Groningen \& Charleroi **Start date:** We operate on a rolling recruitment basis. Please advise your availability to start in your application form. You can discuss this further with your recruiter upon application. **Company Description** We are a leading engineering services company with a proven track record of high quality, customer focused project delivery for over 130 years. Our core divisions include Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Sprinkler and Fire Protection. Some of the other services we provide include Waste to Energy (BioEnergy), Specialist Lift, Off Site Fabrication, Technical Support Services, Maintenance, Asset Management, BIM (Building Information Modelling) and Geo\-Surveying. We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, Continuous Professional Development (CPD) leading to chartered status, performance incentives and the ability to progress your career with a world class engineering contractor. **Job Description** * Review and understand project documents (specifications, drawings etc). * Supporting with design reviews and analysing supplier information for alignment with project documents. * Develop and manage technical submittals, RFIs, procurement registers, trackers and associated administrative duties. * Working with the BIM team to capture all electrical aspects of the project requirements. * Coordination with other trades and disciplines. * Supporting various project meetings as appropriate. * Supporting weekly project reporting. * Supporting QA/QC on site including system walkdowns and project handover protocols. This role will provide exposure to a wide range of expertise relating to data centre, contracting and other sectors. The successful candidate will be supported in developing their knowledge and skills as their responsibilities grow and evolve. They will also be encouraged and supported to work towards achieving Chartership with Engineers Ireland throughout their career with Jones Engineering. **Requirements** To excel in this role, you should possess the following skills and qualifications: * Degree Qualification in an Electrical Engineering discipline. * Valid Safepass / Manual handling certificates required for successful applicants. * Trade background would be advantageous but not necessary. * Experience/knowledge of data centres or construction sector would be advantageous. * Strong team player with excellent interpersonal skills, able to collaborate effectively with colleagues, clients, and external partners. * Excellent organisational and planning skills. * Proficiency in MS Office systems and other engineering software. * Demonstrated appetite for continuous learning and personal development. **Graduate Development Programme** As part of our commitment to your growth and career development, you will be enrolled in our comprehensive 2\-year Graduate Development Programme. This programme includes experiential learning, mentoring, professional development courses, and in\-house training on key skills such as technical writing, presentation, and time management. You will benefit from peer to peer learning and receive support for obtaining professional certifications. This structured approach ensures you gain the necessary skills and experience to progress in your career. **Benefits** Be part of our Graduate Programme and contribute to meaningful projects that drive innovation and sustainability across multiple industries. We offer a competitive salary package commensurate with experience and qualifications. In addition, you will have the opportunity to work in a dynamic and supportive environment, collaborating with industry leaders and contributing to a sustainable future. Our company values work\-life balance and encourages professional growth through training and development programs. If you are ready to make a difference and be part of a growing industry, please submit your CV, along with a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and why you are interested in this role. Recognising the contributions and respecting our people is core to our culture and values. We are an equal opportunities employer, and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply for roles. Please note, we are currently not accepting applications from agencies for this role.

title: Graduate Engineer - Transport 2026
description: Tetra Tech is looking for Graduates to join our **Graduate Transport Engineer Programme 2026** . By joining us, you will be part of a leading global multi\-disciplinary consultancy. We specialise in all areas of the built and natural environment, shaping the future of our environmental, social and economic landscapes. Our projects are all around you. We define, design and manage projects that create shared value to a complex, urbanising and resource\-scarce world. Our people are our success. We need people who share our passion on solving the problems that matter. And where you’ll be supported every step of the way. Here is where you come to build a career. **What You Will Be Doing** You will be working with a strong team of highly knowledgeable consultants giving you the opportunity to develop your skill set and progress your career. You’ll be trained in providing support and assistance across a range of projects and technical areas. You will provide consultancy advice to our clients, contributing towards the success of the team through the effective delivery of project and ensuring development of key client relationships. **Who We Are Looking For** To be considered for our graduate programme you will need to be on course for achieving a relevant degree on one of the following subjects: BEng; MEng in Civil in Engineering. We encourage and value different ideas, perspectives, and styles of thinking. We need a mix of experiences and skills to develop the most innovative ideas to positively shape the impacts of our clients’ projects. We are looking out for people who take opportunities, those who are keen to learn and develop, intelligent risk takers who are not scared to try new things, and who adapt and move quickly. **What’s in it for you?** At Tetra Tech we work as one team to achieve exceptional outcomes and, joining the Graduate Scheme is no different. From early on in your career you will have exposure to sustainable projects. Building the foundations of a consultancy career by growing skills, knowledge, and behaviours; and building experience and achievements into real expertise. We are an accredited training provider – from entry level to master’s degree level professional qualifications in leadership \& management and executive coaching \& mentoring, we support learning and development for everyone. A personal development plan and a transparent career pathway puts you in the driving seat of your career and you’ll be supported as far as you want to go. **Applications** As part of the selection process, it is mandatory to upload a CV and Covering Letter as we will not process your application without one. In your covering letter we want to know why you are interested in RPS and the discipline you are applying against. We would also like to know a little about you as an individual. **What happens next?** If this opportunity suits your career journey, we’d love to hear from you. Our closing date for graduate applications is **Thursday 30th April 2026** . There could be a possibility that we close earlier if we have volume applications. We would recommend for you to get an application in as soon as possible to avoid disappointment. If you are successful, you will be invited to an interview. This will be an opportunity to meet the team who you could be working with. You will be able to showcase your relevant skills and suitability for the role and, ask any questions on how the team works and what to expect in the role day to day. **Recruitment Agencies** We have a Preferred Supplier List of trusted partners to assist us when required and do not acknowledge any speculative CVs or unsolicited candidate introductions from agencies not on this list. Please refer to the careers page on our website if you require any further information.

As a Tetra Tech company, RPS is proud to offer our people market leading development and project opportunities, helping to solve some of the world’s most complex problems. **Applications** As part of the selection process, it is mandatory to upload a CV and Covering Letter as we will not process your application without one. In your covering letter we want to know why you are interested in RPS and the discipline you are applying against. We would also like to know a little about you as an individual. **What happens next?** If this opportunity suits your career journey, we’d love to hear from you. We would recommend for you to get an application in as soon as possible to avoid disappointment. If you are successful, you will be invited to an interview. This will be an opportunity to meet the team who you could be working with. You will be able to showcase your relevant skills and suitability for the role and, ask any questions on how the team works and what to expect in the role day to day. **Recruitment Agencies** We have a Preferred Supplier List of trusted partners to assist us when required and do not acknowledge any speculative CVs or unsolicited candidate introductions from agencies not on this list. Please refer to the careers page on our website if you require any further information.

title: Graduate Engineer Programme 2026 - Water, Gas & Utilities
description: **Tetra Tech** is looking for Graduates to join our **Water, Gas \& Utilities Engineer Graduate Programme 2026** . By joining us, you will be part of a leading global multi\-disciplinary consultancy. We specialise in all areas of the built and natural environment, shaping the future of our environmental, social and economic landscapes. Our projects are all around you. We define, design and manage projects that create shared value to a complex, urbanising and resource\-scarce world. Our people are our success. We need people who share our passion on solving the problems that matter. And where you’ll be supported every step of the way.
