title: Legal PA, Litigation & Dispute Management - Dublin
description: We are Eversheds Sutherland, a global law firm, with more than 5,000 people across over 30 countries. We’re full\-service with deep niche and sector experience. Whatever challenge, wherever in the world, we’re equipped and ready to meet it. We live our values, we’re purposeful and purpose\-led. So although the world is fast\-moving and rapidly changing, we see it as a place where everyone can thrive. We’re ambitious for our clients, our communities – and for you. Whether you’re starting out on your career or well established, whether you’re a lawyer or in business services. If you’re looking for what’s next, we are too. **What to expect** As a Legal PA within our Practice Group Support team, your role is pivotal. You’ll provide confidential, professional and proactive support to a designated group of stakeholders. Confidence, effective communication and influential skills are essential. We value an assertive and forward\-thinking approach. Here’s what to expect: **Diverse responsibilities:** Our Legal PAs handle a wide range of tasks, supporting various aspects of our business. Empowerment and mentorship: You’ll lead by example, fostering ongoing personal and professional development among junior colleagues and peers. **Continuous learning:** We invest in your professional development through our development plans. We offer personalised mentoring and coaching, technical training and soft skills courses to boost motivation and success in your career path. **Culture of feedback:** Our culture thrives on continuous feedback that builds confidence, development and recognition of achievements. **Pro Bono, Community and Diversity \& Inclusion (D\&I) opportunities:** Beyond your daily tasks, we encourage you to contribute to Pro Bono, Community and D\&I initiatives. Make a difference by using your skills to benefit our communities. **Ambition for people, clients and communities:** Our ambition extends beyond business success. We are equally committed to the growth and well\-being of our people, clients and the broader community. **Staying ahead:** Collaborate closely with lawyers, take ownership of tasks and exceed business expectations, anticipate challenges, adapt to changing technologies. in the legal sector. For talented, ambitious individuals there's no better place to be working in the law industry. **Role and key responsibilities** **Stakeholder engagement:** Conduct regular one to one meetings with stakeholders to discuss actions, priorities, expectations and future planning. Attend team meetings to stay informed and contribute effectively. Act as a liaison point for both internal and external clients, draft clear and concise replies to incoming correspondence. Assist with client queries during stakeholder absences. Maintain a flexible approach and adopt hybrid working to accommodate stakeholder and client needs. **Adaptability and collaboration:** Demonstrate client service excellence by being responsive to stakeholder requests. Support the wider PA team, covering for team members during absences or when required to ensure continued resource. **Efficient mailbox, diary and travel management:** Proactively manage mailboxes and diaries including travel coordination. Anticipate future needs and take initiative to resolve conflicts. **Meeting organisation and coordination:** Draft agendas and prepare for internal and external meetings. Ensure necessary equipment and refreshments for both physical and virtual meetings using available technology and resources. **Workflow management and delegation:** Own the end\-to\-end process, be accountable for successful conclusion of all stakeholder requests. Delegate tasks effectively across dedicated support teams such as billing, finance, team assistants, document production to meet expectations and deadlines in line with the Scope of Service. **Document management:** Undertake urgent and short document work. Collaborate with the Document Production Team for larger documents utilising our dedicated software. **Matter management:** Manage the entire client and matter opening process including electronic filing, collaborating with stakeholders and other business support teams. **Financial administration and compliance:** Provide financial administrative support to stakeholders, coordinating with billing and finance teams, ensuring compliance with legal, firm\-wide and practice\-specific policies and procedures. **Pitch and presentation creation:** Collaborate with the document production team to create impactful pitches and PowerPoint presentations for stakeholders. **Marketing and business development:** Coordinate and organise events, create event material, digital working lists and communicate with attendees to ensure a seamless end to end process. **Championing process improvements:** Be an advocate for process changes within the organisation, influence decisions and be a part of the solution. **Efficient administration:** Manage electronic filing, records maintenance and expenses effectively using the firm's designated software. **Technology utilisation:** Leverage the firm’s technology as a key business tool for managing, updating, reporting and research. Create and maintain data sites for efficient collaboration. Seek opportunities to embrace technologies that improve our ways of working and drive productivity in day\-to\-day activities. **Skills and experience** We are looking for: * Experience in a similar environment: Experience working within a comparable professional setting, providing seamless adaptation to our busy environment. * Client service excellence: Strive to exceed client expectations by delivering outstanding service. Anticipate client needs and proactively address any issues. * Problem\-solving and initiative: Be a proactive problem solver. Anticipate challenges, take initiative and implement solutions efficiently. Aspire for excellence in all tasks. Show dedication and commitment to achieving strategic goals. * Effective communication: Demonstrate excellent communication skills when liaising with stakeholders and clients. Your ability to convey information clearly and professionally is essential. * Delegation and ownership: Delegate tasks across dedicated support teams while maintaining overall ownership of transactional activities. * High\-level support: Provide exceptional PA support to senior stakeholders, including managing calendars, organising meetings and handling confidential information. * Relationship building: Cultivate strong relationships both internally and externally. Collaborate effectively with colleagues and clients. * Task management and risk awareness: Skilfully manage tasks with expertise, seeking ways to enhance efficiency and quality. Balance priorities and stay alert to risks, ensuring tasks are completed on time and stakeholder expectations are met. * Flexibility: Demonstrate flexibility and embrace change. Adapt seamlessly to evolving work environments. * Microsoft applications proficiency: Demonstrate intermediate to advanced skills in Microsoft applications including Outlook, Teams and Copilot. * Team collaboration: Contribute to a positive team environment. Offer support to colleagues when needed. **Diversity \& Inclusion** At Eversheds Sutherland, “Inclusive” is a core business value. We bring together different skillsets, global mindsets and approaches. We foster diversity of thought and the freedom to put ideas into action. We have an inherent respect for the individual. We have a strong belief in collaboration and teamwork. Sharing ideas, asking questions, solving challenges and meeting our clients’ goals: together. We want all our people to thrive at work and reach their full potential and we work hard to continue to build a diverse and inclusive culture, monitor and report on our progress and impact, and develop our approach. This is reflected in our policies, systems and processes, and in our work with diversity membership organisations. Many of our people work flexibly in some way and we are open to considering how we can accommodate flexible working arrangements alongside role requirements. If this is important to you, please talk to us about it during the recruitment process. We want you to perform at your best during our recruitment process. If there is any adjustment or support you need, please contact us so we can discuss how we can best assist you. **For you,** **For your success.** **And what's next.**

title: Managing Consultant
description: **Job Description** **MANAGING CONSULTANT** **Dublin** **Fixed Term Contract / Permanent** **Role Summary** This is a fantastic opportunity to join our fast\-growing Consulting Business working with some of the world’s leading financial institutions. As a **Managing Consultant** , the successful candidate will work directly with our clients to ensure they receive the quality consulting services that Delta Capita (DC) offers. The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of financial services, exceptional problem\-solving skills, project management and business analysis experience, the ability to manage, motivate and inspire others, and a proven track record of delivering successful client engagements. You will have deep change management experience and prior success as a management consultant or within a change role in a financial services institution. To know more about our Consulting services, click here Consulting \- Delta Capita. **Job Requirements** **The Role and Responsibilities** This role is ideal for someone with the right mix of consulting and client management experience as well as having an entrepreneurial spirit. You will combine project delivery know\-how, a can\-do attitude, and a strong knowledge of Financial Services. **Project responsibilities include;** * Coordinate delivery of project tasks from initiation to closure, including definition of project scope and objectives, budgetary considerations, resource allocation \& training, formal documentation, delivery, monitoring and control, reporting and delivery assurance. * Day to day management and central point of contact for the project * Manage changes to the project scope, project schedule and project costs using appropriate verification techniques * Create and maintain all project documentation e.g., Terms of reference/ project charters, project plans, status reports. * Establish and maintain governance processes including stakeholder mapping, governance forums (e.g. Operating \& Steering Committees) and all supporting documentation * Design \& deliver documentation to support the successful operational implementation * Identify opportunities to streamline operational process relevant to the project * Manage and oversee project plan and RAID log (Risks, Assumptions, Issues, Dependencies) * Actively manage risks and issues, escalating to senior management and client stakeholders where required to ensure they are appropriately and quickly addressed, documenting as ‘lessons learnt’ once resolved * Build and manage relationships with project stakeholders, ensuring appropriate level and frequency of communication * Ensure decisions and outcomes are formally documented via minutes, sign\-offs, project documentation or any other client specific tools * Define, gather and collate meaningful KPIs and dashboards for management reporting * Undertake other Transformation \& Change lifecycle roles/responsibilities as required **As An Experienced Managing Consultant, You Will Have** * Proven Consulting experience or alternatively in Project Management or Business Analysis within Financial Services. * Change management experience: Strategy, Design, Analysis, Project Management, Build Testing, Go\-Live. * Understanding of Financial Services products and lifecycle events – Trade Finance experience is desired * Risk and control awareness in a banking environment * Experience managing projects and team members supporting successful project/programme deliveries. * Good knowledge of the capital markets and retail banking eco\-system and participants * You will come from a management consultant background or within a change role in a financial services institution. You must have a blend of both Financial Services knowledge and soft skills across influence, listening, presentation and team management. * As an experienced Consultant, you will be expected to be comfortable working on an independent basis and managing your own workload with minimal supervision. * You will also be responsible for helping to grow our business by supporting ongoing operations, RFPs and helping to manage junior consultants. **How We Work** Delta Capita is an equal opportunity employer. We positively encourage applications from suitably qualified and eligible candidates regardless of age, colour, disability, national origin, ancestry, race, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, veteran status, genetic information, or any other status protected by applicable law. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. If you require any reasonable adjustments through your interview process, please use the designated space within the application questionnaire. This is fixed term contract position located in Dublin, Ireland. As the selection and interview process is ongoing, please submit your application as soon as possible. If your profile is selected, a member of our team will contact you within 4 weeks. For this role, a valid working permit for Ireland is mandatory. **Who We Are** Delta Capita Group (a member of the Prytek Group) is a global managed services, consulting, and solutions provider with a unique combination of experience in Financial Services and technology innovation capability. title: Managing Consultant
description: **MANAGING CONSULTANT** **Dublin** **Fixed Term Contract / Permanent** **Role Summary** This is a fantastic opportunity to join our fast\-growing Consulting Business working with some of the world’s leading financial institutions. As a **Managing Consultant**, the successful candidate will work directly with our clients to ensure they receive the quality consulting services that Delta Capita (DC) offers. The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of financial services, exceptional problem\-solving skills, project management and business analysis experience, the ability to manage, motivate and inspire others, and a proven track record of delivering successful client engagements. You will have deep change management experience and prior success as a management consultant or within a change role in a financial services institution. To know more about our Consulting services, click here Consulting \- Delta Capita. **The Role and Responsibilities** This role is ideal for someone with the right mix of consulting and client management experience as well as having an entrepreneurial spirit. You will combine project delivery know\-how, a can\-do attitude, and a strong knowledge of Financial Services. **Project responsibilities include;** * Coordinate delivery of project tasks from initiation to closure, including definition of project scope and objectives, budgetary considerations, resource allocation \& training, formal documentation, delivery, monitoring and control, reporting and delivery assurance. * Day to day management and central point of contact for the project * Manage changes to the project scope, project schedule and project costs using appropriate verification techniques * Create and maintain all project documentation e.g., Terms of reference/ project charters, project plans, status reports. * Establish and maintain governance processes including stakeholder mapping, governance forums (e.g. Operating \& Steering Committees) and all supporting documentation * Design \& deliver documentation to support the successful operational implementation * Identify opportunities to streamline operational process relevant to the project * Manage and oversee project plan and RAID log (Risks, Assumptions, Issues, Dependencies) * Actively manage risks and issues, escalating to senior management and client stakeholders where required to ensure they are appropriately and quickly addressed, documenting as ‘lessons learnt’ once resolved * Build and manage relationships with project stakeholders, ensuring appropriate level and frequency of communication * Ensure decisions and outcomes are formally documented via minutes, sign\-offs, project documentation or any other client specific tools * Define, gather and collate meaningful KPIs and dashboards for management reporting * Undertake other Transformation \& Change lifecycle roles/responsibilities as required **As an experienced Managing Consultant, you will have:** * Proven Consulting experience or alternatively in Project Management or Business Analysis within Financial Services. * Change management experience: Strategy, Design, Analysis, Project Management, Build Testing, Go\-Live. * Understanding of Financial Services products and lifecycle events – Trade Finance experience is desired * Risk and control awareness in a banking environment * Experience managing projects and team members supporting successful project/programme deliveries. * Good knowledge of the capital markets and retail banking eco\-system and participants * You will come from a management consultant background or within a change role in a financial services institution. You must have a blend of both Financial Services knowledge and soft skills across influence, listening, presentation and team management. * As an experienced Consultant, you will be expected to be comfortable working on an independent basis and managing your own workload with minimal supervision. * You will also be responsible for helping to grow our business by supporting ongoing operations, RFPs and helping to manage junior consultants. **How We Work:** Delta Capita is an equal opportunity employer. We positively encourage applications from suitably qualified and eligible candidates regardless of age, colour, disability, national origin, ancestry, race, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, veteran status, genetic information, or any other status protected by applicable law. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. If you require any reasonable adjustments through your interview process, please use the designated space within the application questionnaire. This is fixed term contract position located in Dublin, Ireland. As the selection and interview process is ongoing, please submit your application as soon as possible. If your profile is selected, a member of our team will contact you within 4 weeks. For this role, a valid working permit for Ireland is mandatory. **Who We Are:** Delta Capita Group (a member of the Prytek Group) is a global managed services, consulting, and solutions provider with a unique combination of experience in Financial Services and technology innovation capability. Our mission is to reinvent the financial services value chain providing technology based mutualized services for financial institutions for non\-differentiating services. **Our 3 offerings are:** * Managed Services * Consulting \& Solutions * Technology To know more about Delta Capita and our culture click here: Working at DC \- Delta Capita.

title: Measurement Partner, Global Monetization Product and Technology, SMB, EMEA
description: **Responsibilities** The GMPT Measurement org is responsible for interpreting the performance of advertising on our platform. We partner with advertisers and agencies to optimize their marketing strategies, uncover insights, and build meaningful connections that drive real business results. The SMB Measurement team focuses on empowering small and medium\-sized advertisers across EMEA to achieve success through data\-driven insights, experimentation, and scalable measurement education. We work closely with sales, product marketing, product measurement, and business marketing teams to ensure that every advertiser — from emerging brands to rapidly scaling businesses — can measure and grow effectively on TikTok. About the Role TikTok is seeking a Measurement Partner for the SMB org, based in Dublin, Ireland, to support our growing base of SMB advertisers across EMEA. This role will focus on helping SMB advertisers and agencies better understand the value of TikTok through effective measurement, experimentation, and actionable insights. The ideal candidate is an empathetic and analytical problem\-solver who is passionate about making data accessible and impactful for smaller businesses. The role requires a mix of analytical thinking, client consulting, scaled problem solving, and cross\-functional collaboration to scale measurement best practices across markets. Responsibilities \- Partner primarily with SMB advertisers and their agencies to deliver high\-quality measurement and analytical support that drives understanding of campaign performance and platform value. \- Develop scalable solutions and measurement frameworks in collaboration with revenue, product marketing, and product managers. \- Translate complex analytical results into actionable recommendations that help SMB clients optimize their media investment and drive measurable outcomes. \- Identify and activate test\-and\-learn opportunities (e.g., A/B tests, lift studies, creative experiments) to help advertisers grow their confidence in measurement. \- Manage client and agency feedback to ensure their voice is reflected in internal product, partnerships, and go\-to\-market strategies. \- Partner with internal enablement and operations teams to improve the efficiency, quality, and scalability of measurement support across SMB markets. \- Contribute to thought leadership and internal education on best practices for experimentation, attribution, and business outcome measurement for SMB advertisers. **Qualifications** Minimum Qualifications \- 5\+ years of experience in digital advertising, marketing analytics, or measurement roles within a platform, agency, or analytics supplier. \- Familiarity with experimental design, incrementality testing, multi\-touch attribution, and media mix modeling. \- Strong understanding of programmatic auction and commonly used ad products within the ad tech ecosystem. \- Understanding of how small and medium business (SMB) advertisers approach performance measurement and optimization. \- Proven ability to translate complex analytical outcomes into clear, actionable narratives for non\-technical audiences. \- Exceptional time management, prioritization, and stakeholder communication skills. \- Demonstrated success collaborating effectively in cross\-functional environments. \- Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English. Preferred Qualifications: \- Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative field such as statistics, economics, engineering, mathematics, or marketing science; a graduate degree is a plus. \- Experience with SMB, long\-tail, or self\-serve advertising ecosystems. \- Experience with SQL, HIVE, R, Python, or similar analytical tools. \- Familiarity with privacy\-first measurement innovations and data governance principles. \- Passion for making measurement accessible, educational, and impactful for small businesses. **About TikTok** TikTok is the leading destination for short\-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​ **Why Join Us** Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy \- a mission we work towards every day.​ We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.​ ​ Diversity \& Inclusion​ TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.​

title: Microsoft SharePoint Architect
description: ### **Description** As we continue to experience rapid growth, we are seeking a highly skilled SharePoint Architect to join our team. The successful candidate will provide expert\-level support, administration, and consultancy across Microsoft SharePoint environments both on\-premises (SharePoint Server 2013/2016/2019 \& Subscription Edition) and SharePoint Online. The role combines advanced troubleshooting and incident resolution with the delivery of enhancements and change for our customers. Working closely with business stakeholders and technical teams, the consultant will ensure SharePoint solutions are optimised, secure, and aligned with business requirements. ### **Key Responsibilities** * Troubleshoot complex technical issues across SharePoint, Microsoft 365, Teams, and integrated systems; perform root cause analysis for recurring issues and implement permanent fixes. * Manage and maintain (SharePoint Server 2013/2016/2019 \& Subscription Edition), including site collections, permissions, content databases, and service applications. * Configure, monitor, and optimise performance, security, and availability. * Support migration projects (on\-premises to SharePoint Online, tenant\-to\-tenant, or version upgrades). * Develop and support custom solutions using SharePoint Framework (SPFx), TypeScript, React, and modern web technologies. * Build and maintain solutions using Power Platform (Power Automate, Power Apps, Power BI). ### **Skills, Knowledge and Expertise** **Required skills \& experience** * Minimum 5\-8 years' hands\-on experience with Microsoft SharePoint, including architecture, service applications, and administration. * SP data migrations (experience of Sharegate \- who also has good experience of PP/CE) * Proficiency across the Microsoft 365 ecosystem (Teams, OneDrive, Exchange Online). * Experience with PowerShell scripting for automation and troubleshooting. * Experience working within ticketing systems and SLA\-driven environments. **Desirable skills \& experience** * 5\+ years' experience with Power Platform technologies (Power Automate, Power Apps, Power BI). * Experience with migration tooling such as Sharegate, AvePoint Fly, or LinkFixer Advanced. * Experience with Teams Phone (Voice). ### **About Codec** Established over 40 years ago, Codec is a multi\-award\-winning Microsoft Solutions Partner, trusted by public and private sector organisations to deliver impactful digital transformation. With offices in **Dublin**, **Belfast**, **Cologne**, and across **Germany**, our team of over **300 experts** specialises in harnessing the full potential of Microsoft technologies—including **Dynamics 365**, **Power Platform**, **Azure**, and **Copilot**—to drive innovation, automation, and operational excellence. Codec combines deep industry expertise with a proven track record to help organisations unlock the power of AI and modern cloud solutions Though originally best known as a hardware reseller, since 2010 Codec has focused on building an outstanding software consulting business with a team of experts implementing enterprise solutions using products from Microsoft and Oracle. Our customer base, revenues and average contract values have grown steadily year on year and we continue to invest in the provision of new product and service offerings. Our team is a careful mix of recent graduates and experienced professionals from a variety of diverse cultural backgrounds. We are a melting pot of seasoned consultants supported by the best talent from the local universities. We have a thriving placement student academy and graduate recruitment programme which grows year on year. This company is an equal opportunities employer

title: Operational Risk Associate
description: Pantheon has been at the forefront of private markets investing for more than 40 years, earning a reputation for an innovative approach to investing in secondaries, co\-investments, and primary fund investments, as well as capital formation across commingled funds, evergreen vehicles and customized solutions. Our specialist investment capabilities span multiple strategies across private equity, infrastructure and real assets, and private credit. Through our collaborative and committed culture, we find new ways to solve complex problems together and deliver innovative investment opportunities across private markets. Pantheon currently manages approximately $82\.3 billion in AUM across all its strategies, serving more than 750 institutional and 638 private wealth clients worldwide For further details please visit www.pantheon.com **Purpose of Role** The Operational Risk Associate will be part of the wider Enterprise Risk / Operational Risk function and support the team in further developing and managing operational risks across all business functions. The position will report to the Head of Enterprise/Operational Risk. **Key Responsibilities** * Support the development and implementation of Pantheon’s global operational risk frameworks and policies and procedures for adoption by Pantheon Group entities * Support the ISAE 3402/SSAE 16 annual processes including completion of the document, drafting of controls wording and providing external auditor with supporting information. * Support process and control reviews to identify risks and issues in Pantheon’s control environment. Reviews will include identified areas of risk, monitoring programme and new business processes. * Manage Error/Incident reporting process including reporting to management and resolution of any issues. Ensure monitoring procedures are in line with Pantheon’s risk policies. Direct investigation of serious error or breaches and make recommendations for change to management * Co\-ordinate and assist in providing risk oversight and governance across third party supplier relationships of all Pantheon Group entities * Support business continuity process including BCP and testing programme. * Support to manage various risk\-related frameworks such as the data privacy / data protection, vendor risk, Insurance risk, and assist with any client / business related queries * Support and further develop a centralised library of processes, risks and controls in a strategic risk system **Skills And Experience Required** * Degree level education * Experience of working in a risk function ideally in an investment banking, asset management, private equity environment * Understanding of investment management and products * Ability to work on own initiative and in team * Ability to identify when issues should be escalated for further discussion * Controls awareness * Keen problem\-solving skills, ability to offer solutions to issues **This job description is not to be construed as an exhaustive statement of duties, responsibilities, or requirements. You may be required to perform other job\-related duties as reasonably requested by your manager.** **Pantheon is an Equal Opportunities employer, we are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce so if you're excited about this role but your past experience doesn't perfectly align we'd still encourage you to apply.**

title: Operations Assistant
company: Royal College of Surgeons in Ireland (RCSI)
description: **Title:** Operations Assistant **Department:** Faculty of Radiologists and Radiation Oncologists **Tenure:** Permanent \- Full\-time **Location:** RCSI, St Stephen’s Green, Dublin 2 **Reporting to:** Dean of the Faculty or nominee **About the post** This post is designed to provide a comprehensive administrative support service to the Dean, Honorary Secretary and Officers of the Board of the Faculty of Radiologists and Radiation Oncologists with specific emphasis on the Faculty training programmes. This role is an integral part of the Faculty ideally suited for an ambitious individual to support trainees on their journey from application to graduation along with ad\-hoc events and projects within the Faculty. **Duties include:** * Act as the key point of contact for trainees, fellows, training coordinators, clients and stakeholders * Support trainee recruitment to the programmes through response to enquiries, liaising with prospective trainees, follow up with applicants and management of the online training programme systems * Assisting with the organisation and operation of scientific meetings, functions, conferring ceremonies, board and committee meetings and other events associated with the Faculty. * Assisting with organising and running of training courses, modules, interviews and assessments * Work collaboratively with the National Training Coordinators, lecturers, trainees and other Operations Assistants to ensure effective running of courses and delivery of a highly flexible, responsive and effective service to all * Assist with preparing documentation for accreditation and site visits for both radiation oncology and diagnostic radiology training programmes * Maintain training passports for trainees and ensure these are up to date for all individuals, ensuring all mandatory courses completed for CSCST purposes * Maintain the educational calendar for all Faculty courses throughout the year and updating MedHub with training courses, modules, lectures and recordings * Attend all operational educational meetings and discuss any changes on the calendar and manage and coordinate ongoing requirements for courses as they arise * Preparing training programme specialty rotations, lecture schedules, recordings as required. * Provision of assistance to tutors in the set up of zoom and uploading of tutors presentations for lecture purposes in the lecture theatre/tutorial room * Updating Hospital Buddy with dates for lectures and circulation details for lectures to both tutors and SpRs * Updating MedHub with tutors slides for revision purposes for SpRs * Support the delivery of online modules, courses, webinars and conferences in line with faculty requirements as they arise * Collating programme outcomes into documented reports in support of programmatic review and continuous quality improvement * Preparation and collation of the Faculty Annual Report * Providing assistance to the Executive Officer * Reviewing and updating the Faculty website with relevant information, reports, publications, news etc * Collation and circulation of all information and documentation for meetings. Preparing and taking minutes as required and keeping Sharepoint updated * Assist with the preparation and completion of circulars for Fellows and trainees * Assist with processing of expense claims for board and committee members, lecturers, visiting speakers, trainee discretionary fund in line with Faculty, RCSI and NDTP policies * Coordinate the ordering and supply of stationery and materials for the faculty within the budget limits * Undergo programmes of training and development as may be required from time to time * Completion of the annual mandatory staff courses and training in technology * Complying with statutory legislation and department rules and requirements in furtherance of own and general staff welfare and safety * Representing the best interests of the Faculty of Radiologists and Radiation Oncologists and RCSI at all times * Performing such other duties appropriate to the post as may arise from time to time **Person specification** * Strong communication and interpersonal skills appropriate to audience and situation * Have strong customer service skills and communicate with high level of diplomacy and confidentiality * Ability and sensitivity to operate and liaise with busy and highly skilled professionals with differing priorities, geographic bases etc. * Team player – ability to work collaboratively alongside others to maximise individuals and team effectiveness in order to achieve required service outputs * Highly organised with an acute attention to detail and follow through, giving full consideration to all aspects of the task / project including potential issues and wider impact on Faculty activities and functions * Excellent computerised administration skills * Excellent time and management skills * Ability to make decisions, identify, prioritise workload and meet deadlines and escalate problems when necessary * Ability to work on own initiative * Can operate flexibly within a busy and changing environment; can shift focus when required, act decisively and prioritise several tasks to meet deadlines * Flexibility with working hours when required **Knowledge and experience (essential):** * Understanding of accounting and budgets * Proficient in administrative and educational programmes and platforms such as Microsoft Office Suite, Panopto, Quercus, Zoom, social medial platform X etc **Knowledge and experience (desirable):** * Understanding of medical working environment and legal parameters * Proven track record in a similar role would be advantageous ***We are all too aware that imposter syndrome and the confidence gap can sometimes stop fantastic candidates putting themselves forward, so please do submit an application — we’d love to hear from you.***

title: Operations Risk & Resilience Placement - 2026 Work Placement
description: **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Dublin and Cork.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. The Operations Consulting Team Supports Clients Across The Full Asset Lifecycle—from Acquisition And Planning Through To Operation And Disposal—by Addressing The Interdependencies Between People, Processes, Systems, And Assets. The Discipline Delivers a Broad And Integrated Set Of Services, Including * Operational Strategy \& Transformation: Helping clients align operational execution with strategic goals, including digital transformation initiatives and process improvement * Governance, Risk \& Compliance (GRC): Support the delivery of effective compliance and risk management programmes to identify, manage and mitigate business and digital related risk * Operational Readiness \& Performance Improvement: Ensuring systems, teams, and processes are prepared for launch or transition, and continuously optimised for performance * Digital Enablement: Leveraging tools like BIM, Digital Twins, and analytics platforms to improve operational visibility and decision\-making **The Opportunity** Arup Ireland are seeking talented students to join our Cork or Dublin team. We look for students who are ready to jump into the professional world; diverse individuals with inquisitive minds and a desire to challenge. You will work alongside skilled professionals and contribute to the success of real projects. You will also enjoy learning sessions with technical experts within your team, working on local projects and all kinds of social events. You will be working with a team of Operations Consulting Services professionals from various offices. The Operations Consulting team embodies Arup’s Total Design philosophy by integrating people, processes, technology, and physical assets into cohesive operational strategies that are embedded from concept through to implementation. The team works with a variety of public sector organisations, including Transport Infrastructure Ireland. With reference to our Risk \& Resilience service within Operations Consulting, it exemplifies Arup’s Total Design by embedding resilient governance frameworks, proactive risk intelligence, and compliance assurance into the operational phase of projects—ensuring that systems, processes, and people are holistically integrated. This is an office\-based role, with successful candidates expected to work 5 days per week in the Arup office in Cork or Dublin. **As a work placement student in the Operations team at Arup, you will:** * Support turning technical recommendations into operational plans and project deliverables with defined sustainability outcomes for our clients * Practice creative applications of technology to develop design solutions * Develop communication skills, including report writing and visual representations * Attend and participate in meetings – listen, ask questions * Be involved in discussions with team members to gain knowledge \& experience * Undertake analytical research to develop, document, present concepts and design ideas * Review project documentation, applicable codes and standards to assist with preparation of reports, presentations, drawings and/or sketches * Give structure to ideas – lots of “taking a first pass” at spreadsheets, reports and analyses for discussion later rather than instruction **Does this role inspire you?** We're Looking For Students Who Are Currently Studying For a Relevant Bachelor’s Degree Level Or Be Currently Undertaking a Post\-graduate Level Qualification In a Relevant Discipline, Including, But Not Limited To * Governance \& Compliance Management * Business in Operations Management * Management (Operations \& Supply Chain) * Supply Chain Operations * Business Information Systems * Computer Systems Engineering * Cybersecurity **We Also Value** * Experience of producing technical presentations and reports, knowledge sharing with peers * History of academic research, technical presentations, and technical report writing * Experience with any of the following: GRC software solutions, Cybersecurity solutions, Microsoft Visio, PowerPoint, or Project, or analysis packages * Knowledge of ISO Assurance Frameworks such as 27001 or 55001 and Data Privacy regulations * Evidence of teamwork and leadership in academic or extracurricular field * The ability to prioritise effectively, balance assigned work and deliver to deadlines **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in investing in our people and supporting their growth from day one. As a trust\-owned company, we're driven by our purpose and values, creating a collaborative and rewarding environment for our employees. **Benefits That Work For You** * Professional Growth \& Development: Kickstart your career with our structured program, benefit from continuous learning opportunities, and receive guidance from experienced mentors. * Wellbeing and Culture: At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * Equality, Diversity and Inclusion: We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. If you need any assistance or adjustments, please visit our Candidate Support page for contact information and assistance. We're happy to help. **Please note this role is only suitable for those who are in full time education and are looking for a placement for the summer months or for those who are required to complete a placement as part of their course requirements.** **If you have recently graduated, please apply to one of our '2026 Graduate Programme' roles.** \#IREIntern **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**

agendas are set and comprehensive actions are recorded and tracked \* Assist in tracking project outcomes and risks, preparing necessary documentation for escalation to appropriate management channels \* Support the administrative aspects of managing multiple third party/vendor relationships, including tracking deliverables and vendor correspondence \* Serve as a reliable point of contact for project team members and assist in coordinating communication with senior stakeholders Required skills and qualifications \* Three to five years of project administration or PMO support experience in working collaboratively with cross\-functional project teams and all levels of management \* Strong written and verbal communication skills \* Proficiency in project management and reporting tools (e.g., MS Office suite, Jira, Confluence, or similar) \* Agile Scrum

title: PMO Business Analyst
description: **About Us** At Davy, it's the unique talents of all our people that have been the foundations of our success for 100 years. As we continue to grow, so do you. Because you are not just part of our team \- you are a key player in shaping our future. At Davy, you are the difference. Established in 1926, the Davy Group sits at the heart of wealth and capital in Ireland, providing a broad range of services to high\-net\-worth individuals, self\-directed investors, small\-to\-medium enterprises, credit unions, corporations, and domestic \& international institutional investors. Our culture is shaped around three values, which are at the heart of everything we do: client success, one Davy, and building a proud legacy. Sharing the same goals, we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for our clients. As part of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. **About The Role** An exciting opportunity has arisen within Davy's central Programme Management Office (PMO). As a PMO Business Analyst, you will play a critical role in supporting project delivery across the organisation by helping teams translate business needs into clear requirements, enhance processes, strengthen governance, and enable data\-driven decision‑making. This is a business\-facing role suited to someone who enjoys working across multiple projects, engaging with stakeholders, and helping shape how the firm delivers change. **In The Role, The Successful Candidate Will** Business Analysis \& Process Improvement * Partner with Project Managers and business stakeholders to understand business needs and translate them into high‑quality business requirements and user stories. * Map and analyse existing business processes to identify opportunities for simplification and standardisation. * Support PMO continuous improvement initiatives, including enhancements to delivery frameworks, reporting, and governance. Reporting \& Insights * Produce high quality reports. * Analyse data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities. * Create dashboards and management insights Stakeholder Engagement * Facilitate workshops, interviews, and requirements sessions with business teams and senior stakeholders. * Prepare business documentation including requirements, process maps, and operating models. * Support communication of change impacts and benefits across the organisation. Project Delivery Support * Assist Project Managers with planning, RAID management, and governance preparation. * Support change readiness by contributing to training materials, procedures, and communications. * Participate in UAT coordination and business\-side testing support. Regulatory \& Risk Alignment * Ensure alignment of requirements and documentation with internal controls and regulatory expectations. * Support PMO audit, compliance, and control assessments as required. **Requirements** **About you** We are looking for somebody who has the following background and skills: * 4\+ years' experience as a Business Analyst in financial services. * Strong experience gathering and documenting business requirements and process flows. * Experience working in project or PMO environments. * Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills. * Highly organised with strong analytical and problem‑solving skills. * Experience within a central PMO or portfolio function. * Familiarity with tools such as JIRA or Power BI * Exposure to regulatory or risk\-related change initiatives. * Knowledge of project methodologies (Waterfall, Agile, or hybrid). **Benefits** **What we offer** At Davy, our people are our biggest investment. In today's fast\-moving world, we know it's important to feel secure and empowered. That's why Davy benefits support you through all of life's stages, offering health and wellness, flexible working options, flexible benefits allowance, learning and development opportunities, through to assisted retirement planning. **Health and Wellbeing** Psychological safety, emotional wellbeing and support are a priority at Davy. We offer a comprehensive Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) and mental health first aid. Our Sports \& Social teams promote physical wellbeing and social connection. Reward and Recognition We offer a competitive remuneration package to include salary, bonus, pension and a host of additional benefits to protect you and your family when it matters most. **Growth and Development** Our award\-winning L\&D function supports you and your growth at Davy. We offer a comprehensive range of professional and personal development opportunities. Our culture supports learning, and we encourage you to explore more at Davy. **Positive Workplace** Our workplace is inclusive, diverse, and offers everyone a sense of belonging. We are flexible and continue to support a hybrid working model. You will get to be your best at Davy. **Values and Culture** Exceptional client outcomes delivered by a world class team, underpinned by our core Davy values \- One Team, Client Success, and Proud Legacy. **Important Information** Davy is an equal opportunities employer, committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We value diversity in both background and experience, and even if you don't meet all of the requirements outlined in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. We review all applications received. As part of our commitment to creating an accessible environment, we want to ensure that everyone has an equal opportunity to participate in the interview process. If you require any reasonable accommodations, please let us know, and we will do our best to make the necessary arrangements. Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to us by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role. Davy Group is a member of the Bank of Ireland Group.

title: Partner
description: Banking \& Finance Partner or Of Counsel A dynamic and growing mid\-tier commercial practice in Dublin is seeking an ambitious and highly accomplished **Banking \& Finance Partner** to join their busy and expanding Finance team. The successful candidate will be pivotal in leading sophisticated financing mandates, driving key business development initiatives across the Irish market, and mentoring junior solicitors. This role is ideal for an existing partner or a senior lawyer seeking a platform that offers direct impact, a clear path to equity, and the opportunity to shape the future of the department, focusing on both domestic lending and international financing structures into Ireland. Key Responsibilities * **Practice Leadership \& Execution** Lead and manage a diverse range of complex banking and finance transactions, leveraging expertise relevant to the Irish market: * **Corporate Lending:** Secured and unsecured bilateral and syndicated lending. * **Real Estate Finance:** Development and investment financing across commercial and residential sectors. * **Acquisition Finance:** Supporting M\&A and Private Equity\-led transactions. * **Debt Restructuring:** Advising on workouts, security enforcement, and corporate recovery. * **Regulatory Advisory:** Providing guidance on Irish financial services regulation and Central Bank of Ireland compliance. * **Oversee and draft** all necessary loan documentation, security suites, and legal opinions, ensuring commercial efficacy under Irish jurisdiction. **Business Development \& Client Management** * **Possess a portable client base** or a verifiable track record of significant business generation and a clear strategy for future growth within the Irish financial sector. * **Actively drive** the firm’s Finance Department's growth through targeted business development, attending industry events in Dublin, and leveraging professional networks (e.g., pillar banks, alternative lenders, and debt funds). * **Cultivate and maintain** strong relationships with existing and prospective clients, particularly domestic lenders, international financial institutions, and corporate borrowers. **Team Management \& Mentorship** * **Supervise, train, and mentor** junior lawyers and paralegals within the Banking team, ensuring their technical development in Irish finance law. * **Contribute** to the firm's strategic planning and management as a key member of the partnership. Qualifications * **Qualified Solicitor in Ireland** (or equivalent with an Irish practicing certificate and significant Irish law experience). * A minimum of **8\+ years Post\-Qualification Experience (PQE)** specializing in Banking \& Finance law, ideally gained within a reputable private practice setting in Dublin or a leading international firm. Technical Skills \& Experience * **Demonstrable expertise** in managing complex debt transactions, with proven knowledge of the Irish legal and regulatory framework. * **Deep understanding** of security structures, intercreditor dynamics, and the Irish Companies Act 2014 as it relates to corporate borrowing. * **Proven track record** of high\-quality legal drafting and sophisticated negotiation skills. * **Established connectivity** within the Dublin banking, private equity, and professional services community. Personal Attributes **Entrepreneurial Drive:** Highly motivated and proactive with a drive to generate and sustain a profitable practice in a competitive market. * **Commercial Acumen:** Ability to provide commercially focused, pragmatic, and risk\-aware advice to both lenders and borrowers. * **Leadership:** Strong leadership qualities with the ability to inspire, manage, and grow a team effectively. * Internal Description

title: Payroll Associate
description: Payroll Associate \- Dublin \- Hybrid ICON plc is a world\-leading healthcare intelligence and clinical research organization. We’re proud to foster an inclusive environment driving innovation and excellence, and we welcome you to join us on our mission to shape the future of clinical development. **The Role** As a Payroll Associate, you will be part of the EMEA Payroll team reporting to a Payroll Supervisor/Manager within the Finance Function. The role involves working to strict deadlines as part of a team interacting with employees, internal departments (HR, Legal, and Finance etc) and external providers. The successful candidate will need to demonstrate sound understandings of payroll systems and processes as well as being an effective communicator. * Responsible for ensuring inputs to payrolls are completed and that trial and final payroll outputs are checked prior to salary payments being made * Ensure internal controls are implemented so that monthly changes are captured and processed correctly and that any differences are reconciled and explained * Upload electronic files and input any manual payments to the bank * Manage the Payroll mailbox to ensure queries are answered in a timely manner and filed appropriately * Ensure that all monthly and annual tax, pension and compliance requirements are met * Provide timely and accurate reporting to internal and external auditors * Work as part of Payroll team to ensure that all processes are as efficient as possible, documented appropriately and SOX Compliant. **To be successful in the role, you will ideally have:** * Experience of EMEA Payroll processes preferred * Excellent verbal, written and interpersonal skills with an emphasis on customer service * Ability to work under pressure, to strict deadlines with a high volume of transactions * Attention to detail with excellent numerical and analytical skills * Proficient user of the Microsoft Office Suite, particularly Excel * Positive and pro\-active approach to work, able to work on own initiative * Team player * Good organization skills **What ICON Can Offer You** Our success depends on the quality of our people. That’s why we’ve made it a priority to build a diverse culture that rewards high performance and nurtures talent. In addition to your competitive salary, ICON offers a range of additional benefits. Our benefits are designed to be competitive within each country and are focused on well\-being and work life balance opportunities for you and your family. **Our Benefits Examples Include** * Various annual leave entitlements * A range of health insurance offerings to suit you and your family’s needs. * Competitive retirement planning offerings to maximize savings and plan with confidence for the years ahead. * Global Employee Assistance Programme, LifeWorks, offering 24\-hour access to a global network of over 80,000 independent specialized professionals who are there to support you and your family’s well\-being. * Life assurance * Flexible country\-specific optional benefits, including childcare vouchers, bike purchase schemes, discounted gym memberships, subsidized travel passes, health assessments, among others. Visit our careers site to read more about the benefits ICON offers. At ICON, inclusion \& belonging are fundamental to our culture and values. We’re dedicated to providing an inclusive and accessible environment for all candidates. ICON is committed to providing a workplace free of discrimination and harassment. All qualified applicants will receive equal consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or protected veteran status. If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process, or in order to perform the essential functions of a position, please let us know or submit a request here Interested in the role, but unsure if you meet all of the requirements? We would encourage you to apply regardless – there’s every chance you’re exactly what we’re looking for here at ICON whether it is for this or other roles. Are you a current ICON Employee? Please click here to apply

title: Penetration Tester
description: **What is the opportunity?** As a Junior Penetration Tester, you will support the security team in identifying and validating vulnerabilities across web applications, mobile applications, APIs, and infrastructure components. Working under the guidance of senior testers, you will help strengthen the organisation's security posture through hands\-on security testing, analysis, and reporting. **In This Role, You Will** * Perform supervised penetration testing of web, mobile, and API applications * Assist with network and infrastructure scanning using standard security tools * Retest vulnerabilities to confirm successful remediation * Provide basic remediation guidance to engineering teams * Participate in ad?'hoc threat modelling and security discussions * Assist in assessing impact and likelihood of identified findings * Log, track, and follow up on vulnerabilities using internal systems * Work with product and application teams on remediation progress * Support preparation of vulnerability summaries and status reports * Help tune scanning configurations and reduce false positives * Maintain working knowledge of application and infrastructure testing tools **What will make you stand out?** The ideal candidate will have a solid understanding of core web technologies, including HTTP, APIs, authentication, and session management, along with knowledge of the OWASP Top 10 and common application vulnerabilities. They should have exposure to penetration testing tools such as Burp Suite, Nmap, and Kali Linux, as well as familiarity with SAST, DAST, and SCA tooling. Basic scripting ability in Python, PowerShell, or Bash is important, and an awareness of cloud security concepts?"across AWS, Azure, or GCP?"is beneficial. Strong analytical and problem?'solving skills are needed, supported by clear communication abilities to explain findings to technical teams. The role also requires a collaborative mindset, a willingness to learn from senior testers, and a high level of attention to detail, particularly when documenting vulnerabilities and assessment outcomes. Experience with any of the following will help you to stand out but is not vital in order to apply for this role; * CompTIA Security\+ * eLearnSecurity eJPT / eWPT **Essential Qualifications** * Related qualification in cybersecurity or computer science **More About The Team** Group Information Security and Resilience (GISR) is a diverse, collaborative team with multiple skillsets. With global cyber threats constantly evolving, the team is essential to protecting one of the Group's most important and valuable assets: Information. GISR not only protect the security of the Bank but also enable colleagues to develop secure ways of working and to help them manage information security risk. This is a hybrid role, based primarily at Baggot Plaza or Cabinteely, Dublin. We typically ask colleagues to spend a minimum of 8 days per month working in\-person to support collaboration and connection. Specific arrangements will be confirmed by your recruiter to ensure they meet the needs of the role and team. \#ij\-hybrid **Why work with us?** The Bank of Ireland company culture prioritises work\-life balance with a commitment to hybrid working, along with 25 days annual leave and excellent pension contributions. Family can mean different things to different people; we offer 6 months paid maternity leave, an innovative fertility and surrogacy policy along with working parent and carer supports. Your health and wellbeing is important to us; we offer a substantial health insurance contribution, employee assistance program, WebDoctor and financial wellbeing coaches available. We also encourage and support staff to pursue educational and professional qualifications to grow and enhance your career! **Key Competencies** * Customer Focused \- Self * Better together \- Self * Be Decisive \- Self * Take Ownership \- Self * Manage Risk \- Self We're on a continuous journey to build an inclusive and diverse workplace. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, lived experience, abilities and perspectives. We provide reasonable accommodations at every stage of our recruitment process for disabilities, neurodivergence or medical conditions. If you require an accommodation please complete this form and one of our recruitment team members will be in touch via email. Any information provided will be treated as confidential within the recruitment team and used only for the purpose of determining and providing appropriate accommodations for the application and recruitment process.

title: Portfolio Associate - Leased, DCC Communities
description: **This job is with Amazon, an inclusive employer and a member of myGwork – the largest global platform for the LGBTQ\+ business community. Please do not contact the recruiter directly.** **Description** AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we're the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain \- and we're looking for talented people who want to help. You'll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You'll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you'll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion. The AWS Global Data Center Supply Solutions Team is looking for an experienced Portfolio Associate based in the either London or Dublin. In this role, you will be a key member of the Strategic Portfolio Management (SPM) team responsible for tracking and analyzing the operational, financial and contractual performance of our expanding portfolio in Europe, Africa and the Middle East. Portfolio Associates work closely with lessors/vendors and internal partner teams to ensure accuracy of our portfolio data points in order to mitigate risks and support portfolio optimization. **Our Portfolio Managers** * Are detail oriented * Work independently in an extremely fast\-paced environment with a high degree of ambiguity. Key job responsibilities * Review, audit and process rent in line with internal Spend \& Transaction Policies and contractual terms to ensure landlord/vendor adherence * Responsible for creating and managing weekly, monthly or quarterly vendor and partner reviews * Maintain positive relationships with internal stakeholders and deliver results requiring collaboration and coordination with multiple teams * Onboarding and tracking of payment setup for all new landlords/vendors * Interpret complex commercial contract language related to contractual obligations, SLAs, rent, tax, insurance and maintenance clauses * Review of contract related documentation and abstraction of critical information. * Assist leased team with various other administrative tasks **About The Team** Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn't followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don't let it stop you from applying. Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world's most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating \- that's why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Inclusive Team Culture Here at AWS, it's in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee\-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness. AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, , and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do. Mentorship \& Career Growth We're continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth's Best Employer. That's why you'll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there's nothing we can't achieve in the cloud. Inclusive Team Culture AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, , and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do. **Basic Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree in Business, Management, Real Estate, or another related field * Experience utilizing commercial real estate software platforms such as Tango, Yardi, or MRI **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience reading and interpreting lease language, managing critical lease dates, and conducting lease negotiations * Experience with lease administration processes * Experience in commercial real estate or lease administration within an industrial, commercial, and/or data center environment Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you're applying in isn't listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.]]\>

title: Portfolio Implementation Analyst
description: **The Company** Fineco Asset Management DAC (‘Fineco AM’) is a Dublin based Asset Management Company, and a subsidiary of FinecoBank S.p.A. (“FinecoBank”). FinecoBank (“Fineco”) is a leading European bank with a 20\-year history and a fully digital DNA with a branchless approach since the start. Fineco is one of the banks with the widest products range available. Fineco have developed a truly one\-stop\-solution which includes trading, investment and payment services. Fineco have developed a 100% proprietary platform for trading and investment which covers all customer needs from trading listed and OTC product to investment product such as mutual funds and ETF. Fineco AM, which started its operations in May 2018, is authorized by the Central Bank of Ireland as a UCITS \& AIFM Management Company with a MIFID top up, and it currently has approximately €39bn in assets under management. Fineco AM is a competence centre\- specialised in the management of fund\-of\-funds, single strategy funds, as well as Structured Solutions Funds. In addition to portfolio management activities, all key supporting and controlling functions, such as Compliance, Risk, Finance and Legal, are also performed locally. The company started with 5 employees and has experienced strong and consistent growth over the past 7 years, with a current headcount at 90 FTE’s. Currently Fineco AM manages 3 UCITS Umbrella Funds – 1 in Luxembourg and 2 in Ireland. **The Role** The Investment Analyst supports the management and development of the Structured Fund portfolio through rigorous analysis, disciplined reporting, and effective cross\-functional collaboration. The role requires strong analytical capabilities, attention to detail, and the ability to operate independently within a fast\-paced investment environment. The analyst also contributes to process enhancement initiatives and supports sales with data\-driven insights. **Main Responsibilities:** **Portfolio Management \& Trade Implementation** * Execute weekly trade building and implementation activities within the Structured Fund portfolio. * Support ongoing monitoring of positions and ensure alignment with portfolio objectives. **Reporting \& Performance Monitoring** * Produce monthly reporting on fund positioning, performance trends, and key portfolio metrics. * Ensure accuracy, completeness, and consistency across all reporting outputs. **Fund Launch \& Operational Support** * Coordinate internal and external stakeholders throughout fund launch processes. * Support day\-to\-day activities related to ongoing fund operations. **Cross\-Functional Collaboration** * Work with teams on documentation for fund launches, investment committees, and board meetings. * Ensure timely preparation of materials and support decision\-making processes. **Process \& Workflow Enhancement** * Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives across portfolio management workflows. * Identify opportunities to streamline processes and enhance operational efficiency. **Sales \& Client\-Related Support** * Respond to requests from the sales team related to fund performance, portfolio data, and product characteristics. * Prepare accurate information to support client interactions and presentations. **Key requirements:** **Experience \& Background** * Experience in asset management, structured products, or investment operations. * Comfortable with quantitative analysis and financial data handling. **Core Competencies** * Ability to work independently and collaboratively across departments. * Strong communication skills, including preparation of materials for senior audiences. * High attention to detail and disciplined approach to data accuracy. * Strong interest in financial markets and investment products. **Skills Framework** * Investment Management Experience: 2\+ years (preferred) * Derivatives Knowledge: Basic * Analytical Skills: Strong * Attention to Detail: Strong * Passion for Financial Markets: Essential * Self\-Starter / Initiative: Essential **What we offer:** * **Growth Opportunities:** Unlock your potential with career growth and professional development paths. * **Innovative Team:** Be part of a dynamic and innovative team environment. * **Collaborative Culture:** Thrive in a collaborative company culture that values your contributions. * **Generous Time Off:** Enjoy 25 days of holidays to relax and recharge. * **Hybrid Working:** Benefit from a flexible hybrid working policy, 3 days in the office, 2 days flexibility to be remote. * **Health and Dental:** Comprehensive Healthcare and Dental coverage for your well\-being. * **Perks and Programs:** Access to TaxSaver, Bike to Work scheme, educational sponsorship opportunities, and professional memberships. *
